messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: No way to see what files have changed when using Agent Host ISSUE BODY: --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory. If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:////plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Locations | Path | Scope | |------|-------| | `.github/agents/*.agent.md` | Workspace | | `/agents/*.agent.md` | User profile | --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host unit tests For tests in this area that touch the SessionDatabase, they MUST use an in-memory database, not a real database file on disk. Use `SessionDatabase.open(':memory:')` and see the examples from existing tests. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\platform\agentHost\test\auth-rework.md ===== --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Browse Actions - **"Folders"** — Opens a file dialog scoped to the agent host filesystem (`agent-host://` scheme) --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host system-prompt customization This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host** (ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's `extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Add file change visibility feature for Agent Host",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "ui-improvement", "file-viewer"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a Copilot user, I want to see what files have changed when using Agent Host so that I can understand the impact of agent modifications and track changes made during agent execution.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an agent is running or has completed execution, When viewing the agent host interface, Then file change history/diff information should be displayed",
    "Given a user opens an agent file in the agent-host:// scheme, When comparing with previous version, Then side-by-side diff view should show changes",
    "Given the Agent Host sidebar is open, When hovering over agent files, Then a tooltip showing recent changes should appear",
    "Given a change history panel exists, When filtering by date range or file type, Then relevant changes should be shown"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Create API endpoint to retrieve agent file change history from SessionDatabase",
      "Implement diff comparison service for agent-host:// scheme files",
      "Add change tracking middleware in Agent Host protocol handler",
      "Store change metadata (timestamp, author, description) in database"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Create diff viewer component for file comparisons",
      "Update sidebar to show change indicators on agent files",
      "Build change history panel with filtering capabilities",
      "Add tooltips showing recent changes when hovering over files"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test diff viewer displays correct changes for modified agent files",
      "Verify change history persists across sessions",
      "Validate file comparison accuracy between versions",
      "Ensure agent-host:// scheme properly routes to new UI components"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed", "Diff viewer renders correctly for all supported file types", "Change history query returns accurate results"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
      "What specific view mode is needed - inline diff in editor, separate diff panel, or change log?",
      "Should this apply to agent definition files (.agent.md) only or all files accessed via agent-host:// scheme?",
      "Is there an existing diff viewer component we should extend or a new one to build from scratch?",
      "What time range of changes should be shown by default (last 24h, last week, all time)?",
      "Should change attribution include the specific agent that made each modification?"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "There is existing file browsing capability via 'Folders' action using agent-host:// scheme",
      "SessionDatabase can track and store file change history",
      "Agents modify files in a way that creates versionable changes",
      "Users expect standard diff viewer behavior similar to VS Code's built-in features"
    ],
    "story_splits": [
      "Phase 1: Backend change tracking API implementation",
      "Phase 2: Frontend diff viewer component",
      "Phase 3: Integration with existing agent host UI components"
    ],
    "sprint_allocation": [],
    "context_sources": ["vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Host Protocol documentation", "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Locations table showing agent storage paths", "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Browse Actions mentioning agent-host:// scheme"]
  }
}
```
